hooks.prompt: A git commit is about to run. Assess whether the STAGED changes COMPLY with the lean ADRs governing the touched files — judge whether the code obeys the ADRs, not the ADRs' own quality. Run `git diff --cached --name-only` for the staged files, then `adg lean brief --model docs/decisions <those files>` for the ADRs that govern them (Decision/Guidance/forbids), and `git diff --cached` for the actual changes; for each governing ADR, decide whether the diff obeys its guidance. Report PASS, or a concise list of violations (ADR id · file · what's wrong · fix). Do not edit files; this is an advisory assessment.

A lean ADR record under docs/decisions/ just changed on disk (the changed path is in your hook input; if unsure, check `git status --porcelain docs/decisions`). Review that record: run `adg lean review <changed file> --model docs/decisions`, read the lean authoring rubric it names, and judge the record against it. Report a pass/revise verdict with specific, rubric-anchored fixes. Do not edit files.
